title: NOC 72320 – Bricklayers | GTR Immigration
description: In 2026, Bricklayers (NOC 72320) are essential architects of Canada’s physical landscape, playing a pivotal role in the "National Housing Strategy." As the country accelerates the construction of high-density residential buildings and sustainable infrastructure, bricklayers are in high demand to lay bricks, concrete blocks, and stone for everything from modern condos to historical restoration projects. Classified as TEER 2, this trade combines traditional craftsmanship with 2026-grade technologies like robotic masonry assistants and specialized high-thermal insulation mortars to meet Canada’s new "Net-Zero" building codes.

classification.teer: FeatureDetailsBroad Occupational Category7 – Trades, transport and equipment operators and related occupationsTEER Category2 (Occupations usually requiring college, apprenticeship, or supervisory experience)Major Group72 – Technical trades and transportation officers and controllersSub-Major Group723 – Technical construction trades

duties: Technical Layout: Reading blueprints and drawings to calculate materials needed and lay out patterns for walls, arches, and chimneys.
Precision Construction: Spreading mortar and laying bricks, concrete blocks, or stones to build structures according to 2026 building codes.
Surface Finishing: Cutting and trimming bricks or stones to fit using power tools and hand chisels, and finishing joints with specialized pointing tools.
Restoration Work: Removing and replacing damaged masonry in historical buildings or industrial furnaces (refractory work).
Thermal Efficiency: Installing firebrick linings and applying high-performance insulation in chimneys and fireplaces.
employment_requirements: Education: Completion of secondary school is typically required.
Apprenticeship: Completion of a three- to four-year apprenticeship program OR a combination of over four years of work experience and some trade courses is required for certification.
Certification: Trade certification is compulsory in Quebec, New Brunswick, and Nova Scotia. It is voluntary but highly recommended in all other provinces for higher wages.
Red Seal: The Interprovincial Standards Red Seal is the "Gold Standard," allowing you to work in any province and access high-paying industrial contracts.
labour_market_demand: The 2026 outlook is Strong, fueled by a national housing shortage and a wave of retirements in the masonry sector:

Ontario: Very Good prospects. In February and March 2026, Ontario issued targeted OINP invitations for NOC 72320 under the Employer Job Offer and Skilled Trades streams. Regional draws in Southwestern and Northern Ontario saw minimum scores as low as 53, reflecting a critical need for bricklayers outside the GTA.

British Columbia: High Priority. Bricklayers are currently on the BC PNP Priority Construction Occupations List. Candidates with a job offer and a trade certificate from SkilledTradesBC benefit from targeted draws and faster PR processing.
Alberta: Strongest Demand. Alberta offers the highest median wages for bricklayers at $40.00/hr, with top-tier industrial and commercial masons in Edmonton and Calgary earning up to $55.00/hr.
National Trend: Over 91% of bricklayers are employed full-time. The 2026 market highly favors those who can work with sustainable masonry materials and restoration techniques for aging urban cores.
Why Work in Canada as a Bricklayer?
Lead the Masonry Revolution: Work with 2026-grade innovations, including exoskeleton suits to reduce physical strain and digital layouts for complex stone patterns.
Excellent Compensation: As a TEER 2 professional, your median annual salary is approximately $71,900, with experienced journeypersons in major cities earning over $97,000.
Entrepreneurial Pathway: Roughly 25% of bricklayers in Canada are self-employed, offering a clear route to starting your own masonry contracting business.
Fast-Track to PR: In early 2026, this NOC has been a consistent highlight in Express Entry "Trade Occupations" category draws and provincial nomination programs.
pathways: OINP Employer Job Offer: In March 2026, OINP issued invitations to candidates in NOC 72320 with a score of 53+ (Southwestern Ontario). This is a prime route for those with an Ontario job offer.
2026 Express Entry (Trade Occupations Category): Bricklayers with 12 months of full-time experience (updated 2026 requirement) are eligible for specialized trades draws with lower CRS cutoffs.
BC PNP Construction Stream: Provides priority processing and targeted invitations for bricklayers with a valid trade certificate and a job offer in B.C.
2026 TR to PR Pathway: A confirmed initiative targeting up to 33,000 temporary residents. Bricklayers in the construction sector are classified as "High-Priority Infrastructure Workers."
